intermediate.title: Comparative and Superlative Adjectives and Adverbs
intermediate.content:
📌 Comparative – So sánh hơn (2 đối tượng)
1. Tính từ / trạng từ ngắn (1 âm tiết, hoặc 2 âm tiết kết thúc bằng -y):
Thêm -er + than
old → older than | big → bigger than | happy → happier than

2. Tính từ / trạng từ dài (từ 2 âm tiết trở lên, không kết thúc bằng -y):
Dùng more + adj/adv + than
interesting → more interesting than | carefully → more carefully than

3. Trạng từ có cùng dạng với tính từ: dùng -er
fast → faster | hard → harder | late → later | early → earlier

4. Trạng từ kết thúc bằng -ly: PHẢI dùng more
slowly → more slowly | quietly → more quietly
❌ She speaks quietlier. → ✅ She speaks more quietly.

5. Dùng much / a lot / a bit trước comparative:
He's a bit taller than me. Florence is much more interesting than Pisa.

6. Less + adj/adv + than (trái nghĩa với more):
Peter is less considerate than Marta. (= Marta is more considerate)

7. (Not) as + adj/adv + as:
Peter isn't as considerate as Marta. (= Marta is more considerate)

8. Any / no + comparative:
Your performance was no better than mine. / It wasn't any better than mine.
📌 Superlative – So sánh nhất (3+ đối tượng)
1. Tính từ ngắn: the + adj + -est
tall → the tallest | big → the biggest | happy → the happiest

2. Tính từ dài: the most + adj
interesting → the most interesting | comfortable → the most comfortable

3. Sau superlative dùng in + địa danh / nhóm người:
She is the best student in the class. He's the tallest in the family. Messi is the best player in the world.

4. Sau superlative dùng of + khoảng thời gian / số người:
It was the best evening of my life. I am the tallest of my brothers.

5. Superlative thường kết hợp với Present Perfect + ever:
It's the most exciting film I have ever seen.
⚠️ Bất quy tắc (Irregular forms)
Từ gốcComparativeSuperlative
good / wellbetterthe best
bad / badlyworsethe worst
farfarther / furtherthe farthest / furthest
littlelessthe least
much / manymorethe most
📐 Bảng lỗi hay gặp
❌ more bigger → ✅ bigger
❌ the most fastest → ✅ the fastest
❌ He speaks more fast. → ✅ He speaks faster.
❌ She sings more loudlier. → ✅ She sings more loudly.
White meat is healthier than red meat. Travelling by bus is more comfortable than by train.Comparative: short adj + -er | long adj: more + adj
It was the best evening of my life. She is the most talented student in the school.Superlative + of (time/group) | Superlative + in (place/group noun)

exercises.q: This exercise is ___ than the last one.
exercises.exp: hard = 1 âm tiết → dùng -er (harder)

exercises.q: She speaks ___ than her boss.
exercises.exp: quietly = trạng từ -ly → PHẢI dùng more quietly

exercises.q: It was ___ film I have ever seen.
exercises.opts: the most exciting,the more exciting,most exciting,more exciting
exercises.exp: Superlative + ever → the most exciting

exercises.q: She is ___ student in the class.
exercises.opts: the best,the most good,the better,the goodest
exercises.exp: good → bất quy tắc: the best

exercises.q: He runs ___ than anyone in the team.
exercises.exp: fast = trạng từ cùng dạng tính từ → dùng -er

exercises.q: Florence is ___ more beautiful than I expected.
exercises.exp: much/a lot + comparative để nhấn mạnh mức độ chênh lệch lớn

exercises.q: This is ___ comfortable chair in the shop.
exercises.exp: comfortable = tính từ dài → the most comfortable

exercises.q: Peter is ___ considerate than Marta.
exercises.exp: less + adj + than = so sánh kém hơn

exercises.q: Your results were ___ better than last time – keep trying!
exercises.exp: no + comparative (với động từ khẳng định) = không hơn chút nào

exercises.q: He is ___ in the family.
exercises.exp: Superlative + in + nhóm người → the tallest in the family

exercises.q: My sister is not ___ as me.
exercises.exp: not as + adj + as = kém hơn

exercises.q: It took us ___ than usual to arrive because of the traffic.
exercises.exp: long = 1 âm tiết → longer (trạng từ cùng dạng tính từ)

exercises.q: She is ___ dancer of the three sisters.
exercises.exp: good → bất quy tắc: the best; of + số người → dùng of

exercises.q: Things are getting ___ and ___ expensive these days.
exercises.exp: more and more + adj = ngày càng... (gradual increase)

exercises.q: He works ___ carefully than his colleagues.
exercises.exp: carefully = trạng từ -ly → more carefully

exercises.q: This is the ___ result we have had all year.
exercises.exp: bad → bất quy tắc: the worst

exercises.q: She arrives ___ than her boss every morning.
exercises.exp: early → earlier (2 âm tiết kết thúc -y → đổi y thành i + er)

exercises.q: The weather today is ___ any better than yesterday.
exercises.exp: not any + comparative = any dùng trong câu phủ định; no dùng với động từ khẳng định

exercises.q: Messi is the ___ player in the world.
exercises.exp: good → bất quy tắc: the best; in + địa danh

exercises.q: It was the ___ holiday of my life.
exercises.exp: Superlative của tính từ dài: the most relaxing + of + khoảng thời gian

exercises.q: This restaurant is ___ (cheap) than the one across the street.
exercises.exp: cheap = 1 âm tiết → cheaper

exercises.q: She is the ___ (experienced) teacher in the school.
exercises.exp: experienced = tính từ dài → the most experienced

exercises.q: He answered the questions ___ (confidently) of all the candidates.
exercises.exp: confidently = trạng từ -ly → the most confidently + of all

exercises.q: Today is ___ (cold) than yesterday.
exercises.exp: cold = 1 âm tiết → colder

exercises.q: She sings ___ (beautifully) than her sister.
exercises.exp: beautifully = trạng từ -ly → more beautifully

exercises.q: Is this the ___ (far) you have ever travelled from home?
exercises.exp: far → bất quy tắc: furthest / farthest

exercises.q: He is ___ (bad) player on the team by far.
exercises.exp: bad → bất quy tắc: the worst

exercises.q: The traffic today is much ___ (bad) than usual.
exercises.exp: bad → comparative: worse; much trước comparative để nhấn mạnh

exercises.q: She drives ___ (carefully) than her husband.
exercises.exp: carefully = trạng từ -ly → more carefully

exercises.q: Things are getting ___ and ___ (expensive) every year.
exercises.exp: more and more + tính từ dài = ngày càng đắt hơn

exercises.q: It was the ___ (exciting) match of the whole tournament.
exercises.exp: exciting = tính từ dài → the most exciting + of + thời gian/nhóm

exercises.q: My brother is not as ___ (tall) as I am.
exercises.exp: not as + adj (nguyên dạng) + as

exercises.q: She works ___ (hard) than anyone else in the office.
exercises.exp: hard = trạng từ cùng dạng tính từ → harder

exercises.q: This is Paul's ___ (good) work yet.
exercises.exp: good → bất quy tắc: best; possessive adjective trước superlative

exercises.q: Your performance was ___ (no) better than last time. Practice more!
exercises.exp: no + comparative (với động từ khẳng định) = không hơn chút nào

exercises.q: I arrived ___ (early) than expected because there was no traffic.
exercises.exp: early → 2 âm tiết -y → earlier

exercises.q: She speaks English ___ (fluently) in the group.
exercises.exp: fluently = trạng từ -ly → the most fluently (superlative)

exercises.q: The situation is getting ___ and ___ (serious).
exercises.exp: more and more + tính từ dài

exercises.q: This is ___ (little) money I've ever had in my wallet.
exercises.exp: little → bất quy tắc: the least

exercises.q: He finished ___ (quickly) of all the runners.
exercises.exp: quickly = trạng từ -ly → the most quickly + of all

exercises.q: The new model is a bit ___ (expensive) than the old one.
exercises.exp: a bit + comparative; expensive = tính từ dài → more expensive

reorder.words: She is the most talented student in the school
This exercise is harder than the last one
He works much more carefully than his colleagues
It was the best evening of my life
Florence is much more interesting than Pisa
She runs faster than anyone else on the team
Peter is less considerate than Marta in the group
Things are getting more and more expensive every year
He is the worst player on the team by far
She arrived earlier than her boss every morning this week
My sister is not as tall as I am
The traffic today is a lot worse than yesterday
It was the most exciting match of the tournament
He answered the questions the most confidently of all
This is the least money I have ever had
Your performance was no better than mine
She speaks English more fluently than her classmates
It took us longer than usual to arrive because of the traffic
The situation is getting more and more serious
Messi is the best player in the world without a doubt

passage.text: My two friends, Anna and Ben, are very different. Anna is {{1}} than Ben in most subjects at school, but Ben is {{2}} in sports. Anna is also {{3}} organised person I know – she always plans everything perfectly. Ben, on the other hand, is {{4}} relaxed about deadlines. He often says that life is {{5}} when you don't worry too much. However, when it comes to cooking, Ben is {{6}} than Anna by far – his pasta is absolutely incredible. Anna runs {{7}} than Ben but Ben cycles {{8}} of the two. They are {{9}} friends I have, even though they are {{10}} than most people I know.

The city of Hanoi has changed a lot in recent years. The streets are {{1}} crowded than before, and the buildings are {{2}} and {{3}} modern every year. The metro system makes travelling {{4}} than driving a motorbike. However, the old quarter is still {{5}} neighbourhood in the city for tourists. The street food there is {{6}} I have ever tasted. Living costs are {{7}} than in Ho Chi Minh City, but salaries are also {{8}}. Air quality is {{9}} than it used to be, which is one of the {{10}} problems facing the city today.

Learning a language becomes {{1}} as you get older, but it is not impossible. Children learn {{2}} than adults because their brains are {{3}} flexible. Adults, however, are often {{4}} motivated and can study {{5}} consistently. The {{6}} way to improve your English is to practise every day. Reading is {{7}} than watching films for building vocabulary, but films are {{8}} enjoyable for most learners. Speaking is {{9}} skill to develop because it requires confidence. The {{10}} learners are those who make mistakes and learn from them without giving up.

Of the two laptops I tested, the first one was {{1}} but {{2}} expensive. The second was {{3}} and had a {{4}} battery life. In terms of speed, the second model performed {{5}} than the first. However, the first laptop was {{6}} to carry because it was {{7}} and {{8}}. The {{9}} laptop I have ever used was one I borrowed from my colleague – it was incredibly fast and had the {{10}} display I had seen in years.

Sports scientists have found that swimming is {{1}} form of exercise for the whole body. It puts {{2}} pressure on your joints than running, which makes it ideal for older people. Running, however, burns {{3}} calories per hour than swimming for most people. Cycling is {{4}} popular than both, especially in cities. Of all three sports, swimming requires {{5}} technique to do correctly. Many doctors say the {{6}} exercise is the one you actually enjoy doing. Personally, I find swimming {{7}} relaxing than going to the gym, and I feel {{8}} after each session.

writing_prompts.vi: So sánh hai thành phố bạn đã từng đến hoặc biết đến – về quy mô, chi phí sống, giao thông, thức ăn và con người
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Comparative and superlative adjectives and adverbs
writing_prompts.example: I have visited both Hanoi and Da Nang, and they are very different cities. Hanoi is much bigger and more crowded than Da Nang, but Da Nang has cleaner streets and a more relaxed atmosphere. The food in Hanoi is more varied, but Da Nang has the best seafood I have ever tasted. Travelling around Da Nang is easier than in Hanoi because the streets are less congested. However, Hanoi is by far the most historically interesting of the two.

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả sự thay đổi của một người bạn hoặc người thân – họ đã trở nên khác đi như thế nào so với trước đây (tốt hơn, khỏe hơn, tự tin hơn...)
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Comparative adjectives and adverbs for change over time
writing_prompts.example: My younger brother has changed a lot over the past two years. He is much fitter and healthier than before because he started going to the gym regularly. He also speaks English more fluently and confidently than he used to. He is less shy in social situations and makes friends more easily now. His grades at university are better than they were in his first year. He seems far happier and more motivated than the person I knew two years ago.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về loại hình học tập bạn thấy hiệu quả nhất – so sánh học trực tuyến với học trực tiếp, hoặc đọc sách với xem video
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Comparative and superlative for comparing methods
writing_prompts.example: In my experience, learning online is more flexible than attending classes in person, but it is harder to stay motivated. Face-to-face lessons are more interactive and engaging than online courses because you can ask questions immediately. However, online learning is significantly less expensive and more accessible for people with busy schedules. For learning grammar, I find reading textbooks more effective than watching videos, but videos are far more enjoyable for practising listening. The most important thing is consistency.

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả điều tốt nhất và tệ nhất về công việc, trường học hoặc nơi bạn đang sống hiện tại
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Superlative adjectives and adverbs with real examples
writing_prompts.example: The best thing about my current job is the team I work with – they are the most supportive colleagues I have ever had. The work itself is also quite varied, which makes it more interesting than my previous job. However, the commute is the worst part of my day. It is much longer and more stressful than I expected when I first started. The most challenging aspect is managing multiple deadlines at the same time. Overall, though, I think this is the most fulfilling position I have held so far in my career.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về hai bộ phim, cuốn sách hoặc bài hát bạn yêu thích – so sánh chúng về nội dung, phong cách và tác động đến bạn
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Comparative adjectives, superlative, as...as
writing_prompts.example: Two of my favourite films are Parasite and La La Land. Parasite has a much more complex plot than La La Land and is less predictable in terms of its storyline. La La Land, however, is more visually beautiful and the music is far more memorable. The acting in Parasite is just as impressive as in La La Land, but the themes are much darker. In terms of emotional impact, I think Parasite affected me more deeply. It is, without doubt, the most thought-provoking film I have ever watched.

intermediate.title: -Ed / -Ing Adjectives: Adjectives from Verbs
intermediate.content:
📌 Tính từ -ed và -ing được tạo ra từ động từ
Nhiều tính từ được tạo ra từ động từ bằng cách thêm -ing hoặc -ed. Chúng được gọi là participial adjectives vì có dạng giống phân từ hiện tại (-ing) và phân từ quá khứ (-ed) của động từ.

Ví dụ từ gốc: bore (làm chán) → boring / bored

📌 -ed adjective = diễn tả CẢM XÚC CỦA NGƯỜI:
We're tired. (Chúng tôi cảm thấy mệt.)
I'm bored. Let's play cards. (Tôi cảm thấy chán.)
I was surprised to see her. (Tôi cảm thấy ngạc nhiên.)

📌 -ing adjective = diễn tả ĐẶC ĐIỂM CỦA SỰ VẬT / HOẠT ĐỘNG gây ra cảm xúc đó:
Running is very tiring. (Chạy bộ thì mệt.)
This film is very boring. (Bộ phim này chán.)
Her visit was very surprising. (Chuyến thăm của cô ấy đáng ngạc nhiên.)
🔄 Bảng đối chiếu -ed / -ing
Động từ gốc-ed (cảm xúc người)-ing (đặc điểm sự vật)
exciteexcited (hứng khởi)exciting (thú vị)
interestinterested (thích thú)interesting (hấp dẫn)
borebored (chán)boring (nhàm chán)
tiretired (mệt)tiring (mệt mỏi)
amazeamazed (kinh ngạc)amazing (kinh ngạc)
confuseconfused (bối rối)confusing (khó hiểu)
frightenfrightened (sợ hãi)frightening (đáng sợ)
disappointdisappointed (thất vọng)disappointing (đáng thất vọng)
embarrassembarrassed (bối rối/xấu hổ)embarrassing (gây ngượng ngùng)
shockshocked (sốc)shocking (gây sốc)
⚠️ Lưu ý quan trọng
• -ed: chỉ dùng cho người (hoặc động vật có cảm xúc) – KHÔNG dùng cho đồ vật
❌ The chair was tired. → ✅ The chair was tiring to sit on.

• -ing: dùng cho sự vật, tình huống, hoạt động gây ra cảm xúc
✅ The lesson was interesting. ✅ I was interested in the lesson.

• Câu hỏi kiểm tra nhanh:
→ Ai/cái gì gây ra cảm xúc? → dùng -ing
→ Ai cảm thấy cảm xúc đó? → dùng -ed
I'm bored. This film is boring.-ed = tôi cảm thấy chán | -ing = bộ phim gây chán
The result was disappointing. The team was disappointed.-ing = kết quả đáng thất vọng | -ed = đội cảm thấy thất vọng

exercises.q: I was really ___ when I heard the news. I didn't expect it at all.
exercises.exp: -ed: chủ ngữ 'I' là người cảm thấy ngạc nhiên

exercises.q: The lecture was so ___. Half the students fell asleep.
exercises.exp: -ing: 'the lecture' là sự vật gây ra cảm giác chán

exercises.q: She looked ___ when she walked into the surprise party.
exercises.exp: -ed: 'she' cảm thấy ngạc nhiên khi bước vào

exercises.q: The special effects in that film were absolutely ___!
exercises.exp: -ing: 'special effects' là sự vật có đặc điểm gây kinh ngạc

exercises.q: I find grammar rules quite ___. There are so many exceptions.
exercises.exp: -ing: 'grammar rules' là sự vật gây bối rối

exercises.q: He was ___ by the grammar rules and couldn't answer the question.
exercises.exp: -ed: 'he' cảm thấy bối rối

exercises.q: I'm not really ___ in football. Can we watch something else?
exercises.exp: -ed: 'I' là người không có hứng thú

exercises.q: The documentary about the ocean was extremely ___.
exercises.exp: -ing: 'the documentary' là sự vật hấp dẫn

exercises.q: The children were really ___ about going to the theme park.
exercises.exp: -ed: 'the children' cảm thấy hứng khởi

exercises.q: The rollercoaster ride was the most ___ thing I've ever done!
exercises.exp: -ing: 'the rollercoaster ride' là hoạt động thú vị

exercises.q: She felt completely ___ after working twelve hours non-stop.
exercises.exp: -ed: 'she' cảm thấy kiệt sức

exercises.q: Looking after three young children all day is absolutely ___.
exercises.exp: -ing: việc trông trẻ là hoạt động gây kiệt sức

exercises.q: I was ___ by the ending of the film – it was so unexpected.
exercises.exp: -ed: 'I' cảm thấy sốc

exercises.q: The news about the earthquake was absolutely ___.
exercises.exp: -ing: 'the news' là điều gây sốc

exercises.q: My parents were ___ with my exam results.
exercises.exp: -ed: 'my parents' cảm thấy thất vọng

exercises.q: The match result was very ___. We expected to win easily.
exercises.exp: -ing: 'the match result' là sự việc đáng thất vọng

exercises.q: He felt ___ when he forgot his boss's name at the meeting.
exercises.exp: -ed: 'he' cảm thấy xấu hổ

exercises.q: Forgetting your boss's name is a very ___ situation.
exercises.exp: -ing: 'the situation' là hoàn cảnh gây ngượng ngùng

exercises.q: Are you ___ in taking a cooking class with me next weekend?
exercises.exp: -ed: 'you' là người có hứng thú hay không

exercises.q: The ghost train at the funfair was completely ___ for the little kids.
exercises.exp: -ing: 'the ghost train' là sự vật gây sợ hãi

exercises.q: I felt really ___ (bore) during the three-hour meeting.
exercises.exp: -ed: 'I' cảm thấy chán

exercises.q: The traffic report was very ___ (depress). Everything is getting worse.
exercises.exp: -ing: 'the traffic report' là điều gây buồn nản

exercises.q: She was ___ (frighten) by the loud noise outside her window.
exercises.exp: -ed: 'she' cảm thấy sợ hãi

exercises.q: The view from the top of the mountain was absolutely ___ (stun).
exercises.exp: -ing: 'the view' là cảnh tượng tuyệt đẹp gây ấn tượng mạnh

exercises.q: I'm completely ___ (exhaust) after that long hike.
exercises.exp: -ed: 'I' cảm thấy kiệt sức

exercises.q: The instructions were so ___ (confuse) that nobody knew what to do.
exercises.exp: -ing: 'the instructions' là sự vật gây bối rối

exercises.q: He was ___ (surprise) to receive a promotion so early in his career.
exercises.exp: -ed: 'he' cảm thấy ngạc nhiên

exercises.q: The whole situation was very ___ (embarrass) for everyone involved.
exercises.exp: -ing: 'the situation' gây ngượng ngùng

exercises.q: The children were ___ (excite) because it was the last day of school.
exercises.exp: -ed: 'the children' cảm thấy hứng khởi

exercises.q: It was a very ___ (tire) journey – we drove for fourteen hours.
exercises.exp: -ing: 'the journey' là hành trình gây mệt mỏi

exercises.q: We were ___ (shock) when we heard that the factory was closing.
exercises.exp: -ed: 'we' cảm thấy sốc

exercises.q: His presentation was ___ (interest) – everyone was taking notes.
exercises.exp: -ing: 'his presentation' là bài trình bày hấp dẫn

exercises.q: I was very ___ (disappoint) with the quality of the food at that restaurant.
exercises.exp: -ed: 'I' cảm thấy thất vọng

exercises.q: She found the new job very ___ (challenge) but also rewarding.
exercises.exp: -ing: 'the new job' là công việc có tính chất thử thách

exercises.q: I was really ___ (amaze) when she won the competition.
exercises.exp: -ed: 'I' cảm thấy kinh ngạc

exercises.q: The news about the new vaccine was very ___ (encourage) for scientists.
exercises.exp: -ing: 'the news' là điều đáng khích lệ

exercises.q: He was completely ___ (depress) after losing his job and couldn't sleep.
exercises.exp: -ed: 'he' cảm thấy chán nản

exercises.q: That horror film last night was absolutely ___ (terrify).
exercises.exp: -ing: 'the horror film' là sự vật gây kinh hoàng

exercises.q: She was ___ (fascinate) by the history of ancient Egypt.
exercises.exp: -ed: 'she' cảm thấy mê hoặc

exercises.q: The ancient ruins were absolutely ___ (fascinate) – I could spend a whole day there.
exercises.exp: -ing: 'the ancient ruins' là sự vật hấp dẫn

exercises.q: We were all ___ (move) by the beautiful piece of music.
exercises.exp: -ed: 'we' cảm thấy xúc động

reorder.words: I was really surprised when I heard the news
The lecture was so boring that half the students fell asleep
She felt completely exhausted after working twelve hours non-stop
Looking after three young children all day is absolutely exhausting
The children were really excited about going to the theme park
The rollercoaster ride was the most exciting thing I have done
He was confused by the grammar rules and could not answer
The instructions were so confusing that nobody knew what to do
I was shocked when I heard that the factory was closing down
The news about the earthquake was absolutely shocking for everyone
She was frightened by the loud noise outside her window
The ghost train was completely frightening for the little children
My parents were disappointed with my exam results this semester
The match result was very disappointing because we expected to win
I am really interested in taking a cooking class next weekend
The documentary about the ocean was extremely interesting for me
He felt embarrassed when he forgot his boss's name at the meeting
Forgetting your boss's name is a very embarrassing situation
The view from the top of the mountain was absolutely stunning
We were all moved by the beautiful piece of music that night

passage.text: Last weekend, my family went to see a science exhibition at the national museum. I was {{1}} to hear that my six-year-old nephew actually wanted to come because he usually finds museums {{2}}. But this exhibition was different – it was one of the most {{3}} things I have seen in years. There were interactive displays that were {{4}} and hands-on experiments that kept the children {{5}} for hours. My nephew was absolutely {{6}} when he built his own small robot. My sister was {{7}} with how well the museum had designed the activities. Even my father, who is usually {{8}} by science topics, was completely {{9}} by the space section. We were all {{10}} when the museum closed and we had to leave.

Learning to drive was one of the most {{1}} experiences of my life. At first, I was very {{2}} because there were so many things to remember at once. My instructor was {{3}} by my slow progress, but she stayed calm and patient. After my third lesson, I was {{4}} to realise I was actually improving. The most {{5}} part was practising on the motorway – I was absolutely {{6}} by the speed of other vehicles. My parents were very {{7}} when I passed my test first time. They said they were {{8}} that I had worked so hard. Overall, it was a {{9}} journey, but the result was {{10}} beyond words.

My friend Anna came back from her gap year in South America looking completely {{1}} but incredibly happy. She said the journey had been physically {{2}} but mentally {{3}}. She was particularly {{4}} by the culture and history of the Inca civilisation. Some moments had been {{5}}, especially when she got lost in a remote village and couldn't speak the language. She was {{6}} to find that most locals were helpful and kind. The food was sometimes {{7}} to her Western tastes, but she became {{8}} in trying new dishes. By the end of the trip, she was {{9}} with how much she had grown as a person, and her family was {{10}} with her courage.

Working in customer service can be both {{1}} and {{2}}. On one hand, helping people solve their problems is deeply {{3}}. On the other hand, some customers are {{4}} and can make your day very {{5}}. I remember one particularly {{6}} situation when a customer blamed me for a mistake I hadn't made. I was very {{7}} but tried to stay professional. My manager was {{8}} by how I handled it. Later, another customer told me I was the most helpful person she had ever met – I was truly {{9}} by her kind words. Days like that remind me why I find this job {{10}}.

My first experience of public speaking was absolutely {{1}}. I had to give a presentation to over a hundred people. I was {{2}} when I walked out on stage – my hands were shaking. The audience looked {{3}} at first, but as I started talking, I could see they were becoming more {{4}}. I told a funny story which made everyone laugh, and I felt less {{5}} after that. By the end, I was {{6}} by the positive reaction. Several people said my talk was the most {{7}} of the evening. My teacher said she was truly {{8}} with my performance. I was so {{9}} that I signed up for another speaking event the following month. It had been a {{10}} but wonderful experience.

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả một trải nghiệm đáng nhớ của bạn (chuyến đi, sự kiện, cuộc gặp gỡ) – cảm xúc của bạn và điều gì đã tạo ra những cảm xúc đó
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: -ed adjectives for your feelings, -ing adjectives for what caused them
writing_prompts.example: Last year I visited a traditional pottery village in Hoi An and it was one of the most fascinating experiences I have had. I was amazed by the skill of the local potters – the way they shaped the clay was truly mesmerising. At first, I found the technique quite confusing and my pots looked terrible. The teacher was very patient with me, which I found encouraging. By the end of the day, I was exhausted but extremely satisfied with what I had made. It was a tiring but deeply rewarding afternoon.

writing_prompts.vi: Kể về một bộ phim, cuốn sách hoặc chương trình truyền hình gần đây bạn đã xem/đọc – cảm xúc của bạn và đặc điểm của nó
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: -ed for how you felt, -ing for the story/characters/plot
writing_prompts.example: I recently watched a Korean drama called 'My Mister' and I found it deeply moving. The story is about a woman who is struggling with life, and I was fascinated by how realistically the characters were written. At times the plot was a little slow and I felt slightly bored, but the emotional scenes were so powerful that I was completely gripped. The ending was both surprising and satisfying. I was impressed by the quality of the acting and found the themes very thought-provoking. It was not an exciting show in the typical sense, but it was one of the most meaningful I have watched.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một lần bạn cảm thấy tự hào hoặc xấu hổ – điều gì đã xảy ra và bạn đã cảm thấy thế nào
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: -ed to describe your feelings, -ing to describe the situation
writing_prompts.example: One of the most embarrassing moments of my life happened when I gave a wrong answer in front of the whole class and the teacher corrected me in front of everyone. I felt so embarrassed that my face turned completely red. The situation was made more uncomfortable by the fact that some classmates laughed. However, my teacher gave me an encouraging smile and said mistakes were a natural part of learning. That was a surprisingly comforting thing to hear. After class, a friend said she was impressed that I had the courage to answer at all. That was a touching and rewarding end to a mortifying day.

writing_prompts.vi: Kể về công việc hoặc sở thích của bạn – những điều bạn thấy thú vị và những điều bạn thấy mệt mỏi hoặc thất vọng
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Mix of -ed and -ing adjectives to describe work/hobby
writing_prompts.example: I work as a teacher, and I find it one of the most rewarding professions I can imagine. Seeing students improve and gain confidence is deeply satisfying. However, the job can also be exhausting – marking hundreds of papers and preparing lessons every evening is quite tiring. The most frustrating part is when students are not motivated and seem bored during lessons. I feel disappointed when that happens. But when a student says something interesting or shows genuine curiosity, I immediately feel inspired again. Overall, despite the challenging moments, I am very satisfied with my career choice.

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả phản ứng của bạn và người thân khi nghe một tin tức bất ngờ – tốt hoặc xấu
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: -ed adjectives for various emotional reactions
writing_prompts.example: Last month, I found out that my university application had been accepted, and I was absolutely delighted. I ran downstairs to tell my parents, who were equally surprised and excited. My mother was so moved that she started crying. My father said he was incredibly proud and impressed by how hard I had worked. My younger sister was confused at first because she didn't understand what the letter meant, but when we explained, she was thrilled too. I felt relieved because the waiting had been so stressful. It was an overwhelmingly positive moment for the whole family.

intermediate.title: So, Such, Such a, So Much, So Many
intermediate.content:
📌 SO + tính từ / trạng từ
Dùng so trước tính từ hoặc trạng từ để nhấn mạnh (= very / really):
Why don't you try to understand? You are so stubborn!
You drive so slowly. It drives me crazy.

Thường kết hợp với mệnh đề kết quả: so + adj/adv + that…
He is so good (that) he gets bored when he plays against me.
John drives so slowly (that) other drivers get impatient.
📌 SUCH A + (adj) + danh từ đếm được số ít
Dùng such a (+ tính từ) + danh từ đếm được số ít để nhấn mạnh:
I love her. She is such a brilliant actress.
It was such a party! We had a lot of fun.

Với mệnh đề kết quả: such a + (adj) + noun + that…
It had been such a terrible day (that) I just wanted to go to bed.
He is such a liar (that) nobody trusts him anymore.
📌 SUCH + (adj) + danh từ không đếm được / số nhiều
Dùng such (+ tính từ) + danh từ không đếm được hoặc số nhiều:
The trip was a disaster. We had such terrible weather!
Everybody loves being with Tom. He tells such funny stories!

Với kết quả: such + (adj) + noun + that…
We had such terrible weather (that) we decided to go back home.
She said such nice things (that) we were all moved.
📌 SO MUCH + danh từ không đếm được / SO MANY + danh từ số nhiều
SO MUCH + uncountable noun:
I didn't know she had so much money.
She always cooks so much food that we have to throw half of it away.

SO MANY + countable plural noun:
He couldn't even walk among so many fans.
There are so many things to do – I don't know where to start!
📐 Bảng so sánh nhanh SO vs SUCH
SOSUCH (A)
so + adj/adv
It was so cold.		such a + adj + noun
It was such a cold day.
so + adj
She is so kind.		such a + noun (no adj needed)
She is such a kind person.
so + much/many
so much money | so many people		such + uncountable/plural
such bad weather | such funny stories
It was such a beautiful day that we decided to have a picnic in the park.such a + adj + singular countable noun + that
There were so many people at the concert that we couldn't find our seats.so many + plural countable noun + that

exercises.q: She is ___ a good singer that she should audition for the show.
exercises.exp: such a + adj + singular countable noun (a good singer)

exercises.q: The weather was ___ bad that we cancelled the picnic.
exercises.exp: so + adjective (bad) = nhấn mạnh tính từ

exercises.q: He speaks ___ quickly that I can't understand him.
exercises.exp: so + adverb (quickly)

exercises.q: I have ___ much homework that I won't be able to go out tonight.
exercises.exp: so much + uncountable noun (homework)

exercises.q: She told ___ funny stories that everyone was laughing all night.
exercises.exp: such + adj + plural noun (funny stories)

exercises.q: It was ___ a long film that I fell asleep before the end.
exercises.exp: such a + adj + singular countable noun (a long film)

exercises.q: There were ___ many people at the concert that we couldn't find seats.
exercises.exp: so many + countable plural noun (people)

exercises.q: He is ___ stubborn! He never listens to anyone.
exercises.exp: so + adjective (stubborn) – không có danh từ theo sau

exercises.q: It had been ___ terrible day that I just wanted to go straight to bed.
exercises.exp: such a + adj + singular countable noun (terrible day)

exercises.q: I didn't know she had ___ money. Her house is enormous!
exercises.exp: so much + uncountable noun (money)

exercises.q: They had ___ good news to share that everyone celebrated.
exercises.exp: such + (adj) + uncountable noun (good news)

exercises.q: The food was ___ spicy that I had to drink three glasses of water.
exercises.exp: so + adjective (spicy) – không có danh từ

exercises.q: There is ___ furniture in this room that it's hard to move around.
exercises.exp: so much + uncountable noun (furniture)

exercises.q: It was ___ awful experience that I never want to go back.
exercises.exp: such an + adj bắt đầu bằng nguyên âm (awful) + singular noun

exercises.q: She works ___ hard that her boss always gives her a bonus.
exercises.exp: so + adverb (hard)

exercises.q: He made ___ mistakes in the test that he had to resit it.
exercises.exp: so many + countable plural noun (mistakes)

exercises.q: It was ___ boring lesson that even the teacher seemed uninterested.
exercises.exp: such a + adj + singular countable noun (boring lesson)

exercises.q: She has ___ patience with difficult students. I really admire her.
exercises.exp: such + uncountable noun (patience) – nhấn mạnh danh từ

exercises.q: I was ___ tired after the hike that I slept for ten hours.
exercises.exp: so + adjective (tired)

exercises.q: There were ___ questions at the end of the lecture that we ran out of time.
exercises.exp: so many + countable plural noun (questions)

exercises.q: It was ___ a long journey that we stopped three times to rest.
exercises.exp: such a + adj + singular countable noun (a long journey)

exercises.q: The music was ___ loud that I couldn't hear what she was saying.
exercises.exp: so + adjective (loud)

exercises.q: She has ___ many friends that she never has a free weekend.
exercises.exp: so many + countable plural noun (friends)

exercises.q: He is ___ an honest person that everyone trusts him completely.
exercises.exp: such an + adj + singular countable noun (honest person)

exercises.q: There was ___ much traffic on the road that we missed the flight.
exercises.exp: so much + uncountable noun (traffic)

exercises.q: They have ___ beautiful furniture in their home. I'm really impressed.
exercises.exp: such + adj + uncountable noun (beautiful furniture)

exercises.q: He drives ___ fast that his passengers are always scared.
exercises.exp: so + adverb (fast)

exercises.q: She told ___ funny jokes that I cried with laughter.
exercises.exp: such + adj + plural noun (funny jokes)

exercises.q: I have ___ much work this week that I haven't had time to eat properly.
exercises.exp: so much + uncountable noun (work)

exercises.q: It was ___ an interesting talk that everyone stayed for the full two hours.
exercises.exp: such an + adj bắt đầu nguyên âm (interesting) + singular noun

exercises.q: There are ___ many things to do in this city that one day is not enough.
exercises.exp: so many + countable plural noun (things)

exercises.q: The exam was ___ difficult that most students failed the first attempt.
exercises.exp: so + adjective (difficult)

exercises.q: He has ___ talent for languages that he picked up Spanish in just three months.
exercises.exp: such + uncountable noun (talent) – nhấn mạnh danh từ

exercises.q: She was ___ moved by the film that she couldn't stop crying.
exercises.exp: so + adjective (moved)

exercises.q: We had ___ terrible weather on holiday that we barely left the hotel.
exercises.exp: such + adj + uncountable noun (terrible weather)

exercises.q: I've never met ___ a generous person. She gives so much to charity.
exercises.exp: such a + adj + singular countable noun (a generous person)

exercises.q: The flight was delayed for ___ long that people started getting angry.
exercises.exp: so + adjective/adverb (long)

exercises.q: He made ___ progress in just one month that his teacher was amazed.
exercises.exp: such + uncountable noun (progress)

exercises.q: There were ___ many applicants for the job that the interview took three days.
exercises.exp: so many + countable plural noun (applicants)

exercises.q: The children were ___ noisy that it was impossible to concentrate.
exercises.exp: so + adjective (noisy)

exercises.q: It was ___ a shame that you missed the concert. It was incredible.
exercises.exp: such a + singular noun (shame)

reorder.words: She is such a good singer that she should audition for the show
The weather was so bad that we cancelled the picnic immediately
He speaks so quickly that I can never understand him clearly
I have so much homework that I cannot go out tonight
She told such funny stories that everyone was laughing all night long
It was such a long film that I fell asleep before the end
There were so many people at the concert that we could not find seats
It had been such a terrible day that I wanted to go straight to bed
I did not know she had so much money until I saw her house
The food was so spicy that I had to drink three glasses of water
There is so much furniture in the room that it is hard to move
It was such an awful experience that I never want to go back
She works so hard that her boss always gives her a bonus
He made so many mistakes in the test that he had to resit it
It was such a boring lesson that even the teacher seemed uninterested
She has such patience with her students that everyone admires her
I was so tired after the hike that I slept for ten hours straight
There were so many questions at the end that we ran out of time
He has such talent for languages that he learned Spanish in three months
It was such a shame that you missed the concert last night

passage.text: Last summer I went on a road trip across Europe and it was {{1}} an amazing experience. There were {{2}} many things to see that we barely scratched the surface. In Paris, the food was {{3}} good that we went back to the same restaurant three times. The Louvre was {{4}} a fascinating museum that we spent an entire day inside and still didn't see everything. In Italy, the locals were {{5}} friendly and welcoming that I felt completely at home. We had {{6}} beautiful weather in Rome that we were able to eat outside every evening. However, there was {{7}} much traffic near the Colosseum that we decided to walk instead. I have never eaten {{8}} delicious pasta in my life. By the end of the trip, we were {{9}} exhausted that we slept for twelve hours on the flight home. It was {{10}} an unforgettable journey that I'm already planning to go back.

My new colleague Ana is {{1}} a dedicated worker that she often stays late at the office. She has {{2}} much energy that the rest of us feel tired just watching her. She prepares {{3}} detailed reports that our manager always praises her work. It was {{4}} a big surprise when she told us she had only been working in the industry for two years. She speaks {{5}} confidently in meetings that you would think she had twenty years of experience. She has made {{6}} many valuable contacts in the industry that her network is incredible. I've never met {{7}} a motivated person in my professional life. She gives {{8}} useful advice that I always write it down. I hope to be {{9}} competent as her one day. She is {{10}} an inspiration to the whole team.

The music festival was {{1}} a fantastic event that people were talking about it for weeks. There were {{2}} many food stalls that it took us an hour just to decide what to eat. The main stage was {{3}} impressive that the crowd gasped when it lit up for the first time. Some bands played {{4}} loudly that we couldn't hear each other speak. There was {{5}} much mud because of the rain that some people lost their shoes. However, the atmosphere was {{6}} electric that nobody seemed to mind. We saw {{7}} incredible performances that it was impossible to pick a favourite. The headliner was {{8}} a talented musician that the audience didn't want the show to end. I have never had {{9}} much fun at an outdoor event in my life. It was definitely {{10}} a night to remember.

I have {{1}} much respect for doctors and nurses. They do {{2}} a difficult job under {{3}} much pressure. I recently had to stay in hospital for three days and I was {{4}} an experience. The nurses were {{5}} kind and patient that I felt genuinely cared for. One nurse came to check on me {{6}} many times during the night that I started to feel guilty for keeping her busy. The hospital food was {{7}} bad that I asked my family to bring me meals from home. However, my doctor explained my condition {{8}} clearly that I understood exactly what was happening. It was {{9}} a relief to be in such professional hands. I left the hospital feeling {{10}} much gratitude for everyone who helped me.

Learning to cook properly has been {{1}} a rewarding experience for me. I used to make {{2}} many mistakes in the kitchen that my flatmates refused to eat my food. The first time I tried to make soup, it was {{3}} salty that even I couldn't eat it. I watched {{4}} many online tutorials that my phone battery was always dead. Over time, I have made {{5}} much progress that people now ask me for recipes. Last week, I cooked a three-course dinner for six guests and everyone said it was {{6}} a delicious meal that they wanted seconds. I felt {{7}} proud that I nearly cried. Cooking has taught me {{8}} much about patience and attention to detail. It is {{9}} a satisfying skill that I wish I had learned it years ago. Food has {{10}} much power to bring people together.

writing_prompts.vi: Kể về một kỳ nghỉ hoặc chuyến đi tuyệt vời (hoặc tệ) – dùng so, such a, so much, so many để nhấn mạnh cảm xúc và mô tả
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: so + adjective/adverb, such a + adj + noun, so much/many + noun
writing_prompts.example: Last year I spent a week in Da Nang and it was such a wonderful trip. The beaches were so clean and the sea was so clear that I wanted to stay forever. There were so many delicious restaurants along the seafront that I couldn't decide where to eat each night. The weather was such a pleasant surprise – warm and sunny every day. I had so much fun exploring the Marble Mountains and the Old Town of Hoi An nearby. It was such a relaxing break from city life that I came back feeling completely refreshed.

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả một người mà bạn thực sự ngưỡng mộ – dùng so và such để nhấn mạnh phẩm chất của họ
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: so + adjective, such a + adj + noun, such + noun phrase
writing_prompts.example: My grandmother is such an extraordinary person that I often wonder how she manages everything. She has so much energy for someone her age and is always busy helping others. She is such a patient listener that people come to her with all their problems. She speaks so wisely and calmly that even difficult conversations seem manageable. She has such a generous heart that she never thinks of herself first. I have so much admiration for her that I try to model my own behaviour on hers. She is such an inspiration to everyone in our family.

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả một sự kiện đặc biệt bạn đã tham dự (tiệc, lễ hội, cuộc thi, lễ tốt nghiệp) – dùng so, such, so many, so much
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: so + adjective, such a/an + noun, so many/much + noun
writing_prompts.example: My graduation ceremony last year was such a special occasion. There were so many people in the hall that it felt almost overwhelming. I was so nervous before walking on stage that my hands were shaking. When I heard my name called, I felt such a rush of pride and relief. My parents were so emotional that they both started crying. There was so much food at the party afterwards that we couldn't finish it all. It was such a memorable day that I still look back on the photos regularly. I had worked so hard for that moment that it felt completely worth it.

writing_prompts.vi: Kể về một cuốn sách, bộ phim hoặc chương trình TV khiến bạn ấn tượng mạnh – nhấn mạnh bằng so và such
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: so + adjective/adverb, such a + noun phrase, so much/many
writing_prompts.example: I recently finished reading a novel called 'The Kite Runner' and it was such a powerful story that I couldn't put it down. The characters were so well developed that they felt completely real to me. The story had so many unexpected twists that I was constantly surprised. Some of the scenes were so moving that I genuinely cried. There is such richness in the cultural detail that I learned so much about Afghan history and society. The author writes so beautifully that every page felt like a pleasure to read. It is such an important book that I have already recommended it to five friends.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một thói quen xấu hoặc vấn đề trong cuộc sống hiện đại – dùng so, such, so much, so many để mô tả quy mô và tác động
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: so + adj, such (a) + noun, so much/many for scale and impact
writing_prompts.example: Screen addiction has become such a serious problem in modern society that many governments are starting to take action. People spend so many hours on their phones each day that they lose touch with the real world around them. Social media has such a powerful influence on young people that it can affect their self-esteem and mental health. There are so many notifications and alerts competing for our attention that it is almost impossible to focus on one thing. I try to limit my own screen time, but it is so difficult when everything is online. We need to have such honest conversations about technology that we stop pretending it is only positive.

intermediate.title: Compound Adjectives with Numbers: 'A Two-Day Trip'
intermediate.content:
📌 Compound Adjectives – Tính từ ghép là gì?
Tính từ là những từ dùng trước danh từ hoặc sau động từ be / động từ giác quan (feel, look, smell, sound, taste) để mô tả sự vật.
They have an expensive car. Your idea is fantastic. John looks tired.

Compound adjectives (tính từ ghép) là tính từ được tạo thành từ hai từ trở lên:
a well-dressed man | a part-time job | a first-class train ticket

Các từ trong tính từ ghép thường được nối với nhau bằng dấu gạch ngang (hyphen).
📌 Number + Noun = Compound Adjective
Chúng ta có thể dùng số + danh từ như một tính từ ghép đứng trước danh từ khác.
Cấu trúc này thường dùng với các danh từ đo lường: foot, metre, mile, pound, kilogram, second, minute, hour, dollar...

⚠️ HAI QUY TẮC QUAN TRỌNG:

1. Danh từ trong tính từ ghép LUÔN ở số ÍT (dù nghĩa là số nhiều):
a two-day trip (NOT: a two-days trip)
a five-hour flight (NOT: a five-hours flight)
a three-bedroom house (NOT: a three-bedrooms house)

2. PHẢI dùng hyphen (-) khi đứng trước danh từ:
It was a two-day trip.
Nhưng KHÔNG dùng hyphen khi đứng sau danh từ:
The trip lasted two days.
📐 Ví dụ theo nhóm
Câu thường (noun phrase)Compound adjective
The trip lasted two days.It was a two-day trip.
The flight takes five hours.It's a five-hour flight.
The meeting lasted thirty minutes.It was a thirty-minute meeting.
The baby weighs eight pounds.It's an eight-pound baby.
The house has four bedrooms.It's a four-bedroom house.
The car can do sixty miles per hour.It's a sixty-mile-per-hour speed limit.
The course lasts six weeks.It's a six-week course.
The bridge is two kilometres long.It's a two-kilometre bridge.
The trip lasted two days. → It was a two-day trip.Noun phrase (no hyphen, plural) → Compound adj (hyphen, singular noun)
She is on a six-week training programme that started last Monday.six-week = compound adjective trước danh từ 'programme'

exercises.q: We stayed in a ___ hotel near the beach.
exercises.exp: Compound adjective trước danh từ: five-star (không thêm -s, có dấu gạch ngang)

exercises.q: She is following a ___ diet plan recommended by her doctor.
exercises.exp: Compound adjective: three-month (danh từ số ít, có hyphen)

exercises.q: It was a ___ drive from the airport to the hotel.
exercises.exp: Compound adjective: forty-minute (số ít, có hyphen)

exercises.q: He lives in a ___ house on the edge of the city.
exercises.exp: Compound adjective: four-bedroom (số ít, có hyphen)

exercises.q: The meeting was a ___ one – we covered everything quickly.
exercises.exp: Compound adjective: ten-minute (số ít, có hyphen)

exercises.q: She is a ___ employee – she has won three awards this year.
exercises.exp: Compound adjective: top-performing (có hyphen trước danh từ)

exercises.q: The journey from Hanoi to Ho Chi Minh City is a ___.
exercises.opts: two-hour flight,two-hours flight,two hour flight,two hours flight
exercises.exp: Compound adjective: two-hour (số ít, hyphen)

exercises.q: They signed a ___ contract with the new supplier.
exercises.exp: Compound adjective: two-year (số ít, có hyphen)

exercises.q: The baby was born weighing nearly ___ at birth.
exercises.exp: Sau động từ (was born weighing) = không phải compound adjective → eight pounds (không hyphen)

exercises.q: She completed a ___ certificate programme in digital marketing.
exercises.exp: Compound adjective: one-year (số ít, có hyphen)

exercises.q: The article was a ___ read – perfect for the train journey.
exercises.exp: Compound adjective: fifteen-minute (số ít, có hyphen)

exercises.q: She bought a ___ dress for the wedding ceremony.
exercises.exp: Compound adjective: thousand-dollar (số ít, có hyphen)

exercises.q: The building project will require a ___ investment.
exercises.exp: Compound adjective: five-million-pound (tất cả nối bằng hyphen, danh từ số ít)

exercises.q: He runs a ___ company with offices in three countries.
exercises.exp: Compound adjective: multi-million-dollar (hyphen, danh từ số ít)

exercises.q: We took a ___ cruise around the Mediterranean coast.
exercises.exp: Compound adjective: seven-day (số ít, có hyphen)

exercises.q: The concert lasted ___ from start to finish.
exercises.exp: Sau động từ lasted = không phải compound adjective → three hours (không hyphen)

exercises.q: She attended a ___ workshop on public speaking.
exercises.exp: Compound adjective: two-day (số ít, có hyphen)

exercises.q: He drives on the motorway at a ___ speed.
exercises.opts: seventy-mile-per-hour,seventy-miles-per-hour,seventy mile per hour,seventy miles per hour
exercises.exp: Compound adjective: seventy-mile-per-hour (tất cả nối hyphen, danh từ số ít)

exercises.q: They rented a ___ apartment in the city centre.
exercises.exp: Compound adjective: two-bedroom (số ít, có hyphen)

exercises.q: The bridge across the river is ___ long.
exercises.exp: Sau động từ (is ... long) = không phải compound adjective → two kilometres (không hyphen, số nhiều)

exercises.q: The flight to Tokyo takes twelve hours. → It's a ___ (12-hour) flight to Tokyo.
exercises.exp: Compound adjective: số + danh từ số ít + hyphen

exercises.q: The training course lasts six weeks. → It's a ___ (6-week) training course.
exercises.exp: Compound adjective: six-week (số ít, hyphen)

exercises.q: The apartment has three bedrooms. → It's a ___ (3-bedroom) apartment.
exercises.exp: Compound adjective: three-bedroom (số ít, hyphen)

exercises.q: The meeting lasted thirty minutes. → It was a ___ (30-minute) meeting.
exercises.exp: Compound adjective: thirty-minute (số ít, hyphen)

exercises.q: The hotel has five stars. → It's a ___ (5-star) hotel.
exercises.exp: Compound adjective: five-star (số ít, hyphen)

exercises.q: She has worked here for ten years. → She is a ___ (10-year) employee.
exercises.exp: Compound adjective: ten-year (số ít, hyphen)

exercises.q: The journey takes two hours. → It's a ___ (2-hour) journey.
exercises.exp: Compound adjective: two-hour (số ít, hyphen)

exercises.q: The baby weighs nine pounds. → He is a ___ (9-pound) baby.
exercises.exp: Compound adjective: nine-pound (số ít, hyphen)

exercises.q: The dress costs five hundred dollars. → It's a ___ (500-dollar) dress.
exercises.exp: Compound adjective: five-hundred-dollar (tất cả nối hyphen, danh từ số ít)

exercises.q: The bridge is two kilometres long. → It's a ___ (2-kilometre) bridge.
exercises.exp: Compound adjective: two-kilometre (số ít, hyphen)

exercises.q: The tour lasts three days. → We are going on a ___ (3-day) tour.
exercises.exp: Compound adjective: three-day (số ít, hyphen)

exercises.q: The article takes fifteen minutes to read. → It's a ___ (15-minute) article.
exercises.exp: Compound adjective: fifteen-minute (số ít, hyphen)

exercises.q: Her visa is valid for ninety days. → She has a ___ (90-day) visa.
exercises.exp: Compound adjective: ninety-day (số ít, hyphen)

exercises.q: The seminar lasts half a day. → It's a ___ (half-day) seminar.
exercises.exp: Compound adjective: half-day (hyphen)

exercises.q: The race covers twenty-six miles. → It's a ___ (26-mile) race.
exercises.exp: Compound adjective: twenty-six-mile (số ít, hyphen)

exercises.q: The contract is worth two million pounds. → It's a ___ (2-million-pound) contract.
exercises.exp: Compound adjective: two-million-pound (tất cả nối hyphen, danh từ số ít)

exercises.q: The hospital stay lasted four nights. → It was a ___ (4-night) hospital stay.
exercises.exp: Compound adjective: four-night (số ít, hyphen)

exercises.q: The car can go from zero to sixty in four seconds. → It's a ___ (4-second) sprint.
exercises.exp: Compound adjective: four-second (số ít, hyphen)

exercises.q: The job involves working eight hours a day. → It's an ___ (8-hour) workday.
exercises.exp: Compound adjective: eight-hour (số ít, hyphen)

exercises.q: Her work experience lasted one year. → She has a ___ (1-year) work experience certificate.
exercises.exp: Compound adjective: one-year (số ít, hyphen)

exercises.q: The school runs a programme lasting two years. → It's a ___ (2-year) programme.
exercises.exp: Compound adjective: two-year (số ít, hyphen)

reorder.words: We stayed in a five-star hotel near the beach on holiday
She completed a one-year certificate programme in digital marketing
It was a forty-minute drive from the airport to the hotel
They signed a two-year contract with the new supplier last week
He lives in a four-bedroom house on the edge of the city
The flight from Ho Chi Minh City to Tokyo is a twelve-hour flight
She is following a three-month diet plan recommended by her doctor
We went on a seven-day cruise around the Mediterranean coast
The meeting was a ten-minute one and we covered everything quickly
She bought a thousand-dollar dress for the wedding ceremony
The training course lasts six weeks and begins on Monday morning
He attended a two-day workshop on public speaking last month
They rented a two-bedroom apartment in the city centre for a year
The article was a fifteen-minute read perfect for the train journey
She has a ninety-day visa that allows her to stay in the country
It is a two-million-pound building project in the centre of town
The race is a twenty-six-mile marathon through the city streets
She has a one-year work experience certificate from a top company
The bridge across the river is a two-kilometre structure
The concert was a three-hour show and it was absolutely incredible

passage.text: Last summer, my family went on a {{1}} road trip across Vietnam. We started in Hanoi and ended in Ho Chi Minh City, driving through some of the most beautiful landscapes I have ever seen. On the first day, we took a {{2}} cruise in Ha Long Bay, which was one of the highlights of the trip. The boat was small but comfortable, with {{3}} cabins and a lovely restaurant on deck. We stopped at several islands and went kayaking for about an hour. From Ha Long, we drove south for {{4}} before stopping in Hue. We spent two nights in a {{5}} boutique hotel there – it had only twelve rooms but the service was excellent. In Da Nang, we visited the Marble Mountains, which required a {{6}} climb to reach the top. The views were worth every step. The final leg of the journey was a {{7}} bus ride from Da Nang to Ho Chi Minh City. All in all, it was an unforgettable {{8}} adventure.

My cousin has just started a new job at a {{1}} technology company. The role is a {{2}} contract to begin with, but there is a possibility of a permanent position afterwards. The office is a {{3}} commute from her apartment, which she does by bicycle every day. She works an {{4}} day from nine to six, with a one-hour lunch break. The building itself is impressive – a {{5}} tower in the heart of the financial district. Her team has just been given a {{6}} project to complete before the end of the year. She also has to attend a {{7}} training programme next month to learn the company's systems. Despite the challenges, she says it is a {{8}} opportunity that she couldn't turn down.

Training for a marathon is no small undertaking. I am currently following a {{1}} training plan that my coach designed specifically for me. Most days involve a {{2}} morning run followed by strength exercises in the afternoon. On Sundays, I do a longer {{3}} run to build endurance. I am also attending a {{4}} nutrition workshop to learn about eating for performance. Last weekend, I completed a {{5}} race as a warm-up event and finished in under two hours. My goal is to run the full {{6}} marathon in under four hours. I have already booked a {{7}} stay in the host city, where the race will be held. My coach says that with consistent training, a {{8}} improvement in my time is very achievable.

We are planning to renovate our house and it is proving to be quite a project. We have just bought a {{1}} family home in the suburbs and it needs a lot of work. The architect has given us a {{2}} plan to complete all the renovations. The main changes include converting the garage into a {{3}} studio and adding a {{4}} extension at the back of the house. The builders say the main work will take about three months – it will be a {{5}} process. We have set a {{6}} budget for the whole project and we are hoping not to go over it. The work will begin with a {{7}} deep clean of the entire property. We are all very excited, though living in a building site will be quite a {{8}} challenge.

The new high-speed train service between the two cities is a remarkable achievement. It is a {{1}} project that took engineers nearly a decade to complete. The train itself can travel at a {{2}} speed, making the journey much faster than before. The {{3}} route passes through tunnels and over bridges, including one {{4}} viaduct that has become a landmark in the region. Each train has {{5}} carriages and can carry over five hundred passengers. There is even a {{6}} restaurant car in the middle of the train. Passengers also get access to {{7}} wi-fi throughout the entire journey. The first {{8}} trial run took place last spring and was declared a complete success by government officials.

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả một chuyến đi hoặc kỳ nghỉ mà bạn đã lên kế hoạch hoặc đã thực hiện – dùng nhiều compound adjectives với số để mô tả chi tiết
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Compound adjectives with numbers for time, distance, accommodation
writing_prompts.example: Last year I went on a two-week backpacking trip through Southeast Asia. The journey began with a twelve-hour flight from London to Bangkok, followed by a forty-minute taxi ride to the city centre. I stayed in a comfortable four-star hostel with a rooftop pool. From Bangkok, I took a six-hour train to Chiang Mai, where I joined a three-day elephant sanctuary tour. The experience included a thirty-minute guided walk through the jungle every morning. I then flew to Vietnam for a five-night stay in Hanoi before heading south by bus on a twenty-hour overnight journey. It was an unforgettable three-country adventure.

writing_prompts.vi: Kể về một dự án lớn tại nơi làm việc, trường học hoặc ở nhà – mô tả các giai đoạn và thời gian bằng compound adjectives
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Compound adjectives with numbers for duration, scale, and specifications
writing_prompts.example: Earlier this year, our team completed a three-month project to redesign our company website. It began with a two-day planning workshop where we mapped out the whole process. We divided the work into a four-stage process, with each stage taking about two weeks. I was responsible for writing a twenty-page report on user experience research. We had weekly one-hour check-in meetings with the client to share progress. The final thirty-minute presentation to senior management went very well. The project required a five-person team working sometimes in ten-hour shifts to meet the six-week deadline.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một căn hộ hoặc ngôi nhà lý tưởng mà bạn muốn có trong tương lai – mô tả kích thước, phòng, và các đặc điểm bằng compound adjectives
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Compound adjectives with numbers to describe property features
writing_prompts.example: My ideal home would be a four-bedroom house in a quiet neighbourhood within a thirty-minute commute of the city centre. It would have a large open-plan kitchen and a fifty-square-metre garden perfect for entertaining. I would want a two-car garage and a dedicated home office for working remotely. The house would ideally be a two-storey building with large windows and plenty of natural light. A five-minute walk from a good school would be important if I have children. It doesn't need to be a million-pound property, but it should feel spacious and comfortable for a family of four.

writing_prompts.vi: Kể về một người nổi tiếng hoặc nhân vật thể thao mà bạn ngưỡng mộ – mô tả sự nghiệp của họ bằng compound adjectives với số
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Compound adjectives with numbers for career facts, records, achievements
writing_prompts.example: One of the athletes I most admire is Serena Williams, who had an extraordinary thirty-year professional career in tennis. She won the one-hundred-metre equivalent of tennis – the Wimbledon final – on multiple occasions. She is a twenty-three-time Grand Slam champion, which is a record-breaking achievement in women's tennis. Off the court, she has built a multi-million-dollar business empire and become a globally recognised brand ambassador. She once competed in a two-hour match just weeks after giving birth, showing incredible dedication. Her story is a true example of what can be achieved through a twenty-year commitment to excellence.

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả một sự kiện đặc biệt (lễ hội, cuộc thi, triển lãm) mà bạn đã tham gia hoặc muốn tham gia – dùng compound adjectives để mô tả chi tiết thực tế
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Compound adjectives with numbers for event details, schedules, and venues
writing_prompts.example: Last spring I attended a three-day music and arts festival held in a beautiful countryside location two hours from the city. The main stage was a ten-thousand-seat venue with state-of-the-art sound systems. Each day started with a half-day programme of workshops covering everything from painting to street dance. The headline act on the final night was a two-hour performance that left the entire audience speechless. There was also a five-kilometre morning run on the second day for those who wanted to stay active. Waiting times for food stalls were sometimes a thirty-minute queue, but the variety was worth it. It is now a twenty-year-old tradition and one of the most popular events in the region.
